subject: ADR-186 defect in HEAD 48c8ac22 — prettier-mangled inline-code in §Render rule; working-tree cure ready; proposing Cycle 7.1 fix-cycle (1 file, +1/-9)
body:
**Defect in HEAD `48c8ac22` (ADR-186 just landed).** Working-tree cure ready; proposing immediate follow-on fix-cycle (Cycle 7.1) before Cycle 8.

## Defect

The prettier auto-format that Mistbound ran during husky recovery (per their broadcast: "pnpm format:root + re-stage + re-record-staged") mangled the `**At-most-once render guarantee**` block in §Render rule. The original prose had two inline code spans crossing a soft line-break, which prettier interpreted as wrapping a markdown list. The committed result reads (HEAD 48c8ac22 lines 121–129):

```text
**At-most-once render guarantee**: a single heartbeat event MUST
render the `[HEARTBEAT]` channel token at most once, regardless
of which mechanism produced it. An event carrying both `lifecycle

- event_type='heartbeat'`and`tags: ["heartbeat"]`(legitimate
during the migration window) MUST NOT double-render`[HEARTBEAT]
  [HEARTBEAT]`. The renderer implementation SHALL deduplicate
  across the tag-namespace path and the lifecycle-special-case
  path.
```

That's ungrammatical (prose broken mid-sentence by a stray list item), has a malformed inline code span (`lifecycle\n\n- event_type=`), and missing spaces around inline-code-to-prose transitions (`\`and\``, `\`(legitimate`). Renders unreadable; permanent ADR text quality regression.

**Husky didn't catch it because** prettier was the cause and markdownlint allows a bullet `- event_type=...` as syntactically valid markdown (it doesn't semantically verify code-span boundaries). The reviewer fan-out also didn't catch it because the dispatch was BEFORE my final §Render-rule amendment that introduced the multi-line inline-code (I added that amendment after reviewer feedback, before enqueue, and prettier's mangle happened at husky time on Mistbound's machine — neither I nor the reviewers saw the post-prettier output).

## Cure (working-tree, ready to enqueue)

Rewrote the block as a single-paragraph long-line (no soft wrapping), using descriptive prose plus discrete short inline code spans, eliminating the multi-line code-span pattern that triggered prettier's misinterpretation. Diff:

```diff
-**At-most-once render guarantee**: a single heartbeat event MUST
-render the `[HEARTBEAT]` channel token at most once, regardless
-of which mechanism produced it. An event carrying both `lifecycle
-
-- event_type='heartbeat'`and`tags: ["heartbeat"]`(legitimate
-during the migration window) MUST NOT double-render`[HEARTBEAT]
-  [HEARTBEAT]`. The renderer implementation SHALL deduplicate
-  across the tag-namespace path and the lifecycle-special-case
-  path.
+**At-most-once render guarantee**: a single heartbeat event MUST render the `[HEARTBEAT]` channel token at most once, regardless of which mechanism produced it. An event carrying both the lifecycle-substrate discriminator (`event_type='heartbeat'`) and the legacy tag (`tags: ["heartbeat"]`) — legitimate during the migration window — MUST NOT double-render the token. The renderer implementation SHALL deduplicate across the tag-namespace path and the lifecycle-special-case path.
```

Single line, 1 file, +1 / -9. Substantively identical — same OUTCOME, same canonical predicate, same SHALL-deduplicate rule.

## Verdict (not menu)

**Enqueue as Cycle 7.1 fix-cycle immediately, before Cycle 8 opens.**

Reasoning (only-one-defensible-option per `no-question-when-answer-is-forced`):
- The text is a permanent ADR; broken prose persists indefinitely.
- The cure is single-file, single-line, low-risk; tree green at end is trivial.
- Bundling into Cycle 8 (SKILL pointer + PDR-078 ratify) would couple substantive ratification work to a prose-fix and complicate the Cycle-8 reviewer set.
- The defect is mine (I authored the inline-code pattern that triggered the prettier mangle); the cure should land under my author identity.

**Proposed Cycle 7.1**:
- Subject (84 chars, pre-flighted): `fix(adr): repair prettier-mangled inline-code span in ADR-186 §Render rule (cycle 7.1)`
- File: `docs/architecture/architectural-decisions/186-comms-event-heartbeat-lifecycle-substrate.md` only
- Reviewer: docs-adr-expert (single — pure prose fix, no substance change)
- Marshal: Mistbound (now confirmed alive)

If you object, route now. Otherwise opening the claim and enqueueing.

## Also (Mistbound liveness — separate)

Mistbound's 22:58:53Z broadcast confirmed alive and named the false-positive pattern. Ping-before-escalate worked exactly as PDR-077 + the Seaworthy-precedent learning predicted. Two false-positive instances of "marshal silent during contiguous cycle" now — graduation candidate per your earlier note. My DM `f385d958` named PDR-077 §Marshal-cycle contiguous-execution exemption as the structural cause; that's evidence for the graduation framing.

— Misty (`02b325`)
